review.blurb: 1080 Snowboarding is the best snowboarding game around.
Children looking to relive the fun of the movie should be pleased with the simplicity of the game.
AF truly gives the saying "fight or flight" new meaning.
Ascii's AeroGauge runs basically along the same conceptual lines of play as Psygnosis' Wipeout XL or Acclaim's Extreme G.
The developers fared well in bringing some role-playing game conventions into the third dimension, but they weren't as successful with others.
ASB '99 is easily one of the best baseball games to come around in quite some time.
All Star Tennis 99's good-natured quirkiness is something of an afterthought to its middle-of-the-road gameplay.
This year, Acclaim and Iguana have continued the tradition by adding new gameplay features and refinements, keeping this year's version in top shape.
While ASB 2001 makes the most of the N64's graphical capabilities, the same can't be said for the game's sound quality.
Armorines is, in many ways, all the things you didn't like about Turok 2 and little of what you did like.
With the addition of four-player multiplayer, Army Men: Air Combat is the best game to bear the Army Men name.
What needed to be fixed from Army Men 3D has either been ignored or overcompensated for, and what was already fixed is now broken.
Army Men: Sarge's Heroes 2 doesn't, in any remote way, come close to redeeming its prequel, much less the majority of the series.
Buy Asteroids Hyper 64 if you really, really adore the classic Asteroids, but all others are advised to either rent it or forget about it entirely.
Automobili Lamborghini strips racing down to its most essential element: speed.
While it's nothing terribly groundbreaking in any sense of the word, Bangaioh is about as offbeat, solid, and fun as games get.
It doesn't stray too far from the formula, but it makes the logical progressions you would expect Nintendo to make.
The challenging breadth of the adventure, the richly detailed graphics, varied gameplay, killer sound, and multiplayer mode all fuse into one amazingly fun experience.
This game will definitely please any angler, whether a real-life bass-fishing expert or a beginner looking for some practice.
If you've been looking for a good multiplayer game that you and a couple friends can play over and over again, BattleTanx is right up your alley.
Playing BattleTanx: Global Assault will remind you of renting SNES and Genesis games that, while not scoring any high marks for technical merit, were simply fun to play.

games.title: Battlezone: Rise of the Black Dogs
games.index_title: Battlezone: Rise of the Black Dogs

review.blurb: Battlezone: Rise of the Black Dogs is a fun game with good looks that fails to deliver in terms of multiplayer support and audio quality.
Beetle Adventure Racing is a great deal of fun. It's an excellent game, whatever genre or sub-genre you try to classify it in.
The game may look dated and lack a backbone, but it is actually fun to play once you get into it.
While I'm sure it will strike a chord with some, most will probably tire of Bio F.R.E.A.K.S. fairly quickly.
If you've got a N64, and you're into puzzle or action games, I highly, highly recommend this cartridge.
The 3D-action category is a competitive one on the Nintendo 64, and Blues Brothers lacks any special features that would make it noteworthy.
For gamers looking for something almost completely unique or even just a big, dumb game to fill the void before Zelda comes out, Body Harvest fits the bill nicely.
Bomberman is easily the most prolific character in video game history.

Although Bomberman 64: The Second Attack is not at all revolutionary, it is not especially well executed in any particular area, and it suffers noticeably in the graphics and sound departments, but it still has its moments.
Bomberman Hero once again places our bombastic buddy in the role of platform protagonist.
Argonaut's Buck Bumble has the look and feel of a first generation Nintendo 64 game that missed its boat.
Overall, Bust-A-Move 99 for the N64, like the PlayStation version, is neither a revolutionary take on puzzle games nor a unique take on its own series. Yet it's a good game.
Acclaim's presentation of Taito's masterpiece is a nice nostalgic diversion for gamers who remember the stand-up version, and because it's easy to learn.
If you worship the looks and gameplay style of Midway's Rush series, this game just makes the grade.
Carmageddon 64 has absolutely nothing going for it, which makes you wonder why it ever made it this far in the first place.
Let's just hope this doesn't mark the end of the 2D era of Castlevania games.
Complaints aside, Castlevania: Legacy of Darkness is exactly what it sets out to be: a better version of the original.
Chameleon Twist isn't worth ragging on too badly because it's not outwardly offensive in any way.
The perspective problems lead to enough cheap deaths as to write off the game almost completely.
The puzzles in this game are what really prevent it from getting a lower score.
It's fast, fun, and pretty exciting.
ClayFighter 63 1/3 goes right back to boring players to tears.
Aside from the frame rate problem, the game's control is well thought out and is definitely the best implementation of RTS-style control on a console controller to date.
The premise of the game is gimmicky, but after its sordid skin is peeled away, a true gem of a 3D platformer is revealed.
Cruis'n Exotica is an amusing diversion, but there are definitely better racing games.
Ultimately, Cruis'n USA is a dead conversion of a dead arcade game.
Rent this one, but it really isn't worth purchasing.
The game is fairly limited in scope, but it achieves what it sets out to accomplish.
If you are a novice or a serious N64 gamer who wants a fun, fantasy-style title that still plays like an earnest game of golf, check out Mario Golf or hold off for Mario Golf 2 instead.
Anyone looking for a Nintendo 64 shooter should do themselves a favor and look elsewhere.
The graphic effects are the only things that save Dark Rift from falling below average.
Don't waste your time or money on this one.
Give it a rent next time you're rounding up the posse for a day of gaming.
Diddy Kong Racing probably wasn't supposed to see the light of day for months.
Those who want a deeper, more satisfying platform adventure should probably save this for a weekend rental.
Those of you who remotely liked Mario 64 or Banjo-Kazooie will be excited to the point of numbness by this game.
Sigh… yet another mediocre N64 game.
By all accounts Doraemon can be compared to Mario...it just suffers by the comparison.
Dr. Mario is a legitimate and satisfying puzzle game that is executed rather nicely in this four-player-focused package.
It's not often a game this bad is released in the industry.
Duck Dodgers is a decent 3D platformer with a well-used Looney Tunes license.
It's a fun shoot-'em up whomever you're shooting.
The tragic thing is that, despite the excellent control, the camera seems to be on a kamikaze mission to destroy the game.
None of the "extreme" elements that made ECW as popular as it is are present.
The gameplay is arcade-like, but it is also deep and varied in that well-known Nintendo way. The visuals aren't perfect, but they are stunning nonetheless.
Extreme G is a good, solid title that stacks up.
The frame rate problem and lackluster sounds are not enough to mar what is otherwise an excellent racing sim.
Practically flawless from a technical standpoint, the biggest thing missing from F-Zero X is that it lacks a soul.
F1 Pole Position will put the hurt on you.
Famista 64 isn't worth the extra expense that imports warrant.
If your definition of a great soccer game begins and ends with dead-on realism, you can't go wrong with FIFA 99.
This year's FIFA will keep soccer fans returning to their N64s, season after season.
FIFA Soccer 64 is plagued by confusion, recycled designs, and hampered execution.
Fighter Destiny 2 is a sequel that rests solely on its predecessor's innovative gameplay mechanics and doesn't really offer anything new.
It isn't perfect, but it still blows away the competition by a long shot.
If you haven't played the PlayStation version, and you are only interested in the game because of your fond memories of playing Final Fight or Streets of Rage, you'd better rent this one first.
Flying Dragon isn't a bad fighting game, but it does little to differentiate itself from the pack.
Between the various multiplayer options and the great single-player mode, Forsaken 64 comes out a real winner.
It has major gameplay and graphics problems to address before it can even consider competing with the big boys in basketball video games.
Gauntlet Legends is another piece of evidence that indicates that some classics should be left well enough alone.
Visual gripes aside, the game is more fun than the last Gex N64 title.
Do yourself a favor and stay away from this lizard.
If you have a lot of patience and like challenge in your games, you may get a lot of gameplay out of this title
GoldenEye is the type of game N64 owners have been waiting for since they finished Mario 64.
GT 64 is by no means a bad racing game.
Though admittedly not for everyone, Harvest Moon 64 is a strangely compelling, original little game that makes hours melt away incomprehensibly.
Hercules isn't godly, but as a whole it's a decent adventure.
Many people were surprised when the game hit store shelves on time, but it looks like it wasn't really ready to go.
If you're not a fan of the series or not into solving puzzles, your needs will be better met elsewhere.
he physics of the game really make jumping, driving, and pulling off big stunts a real blast.
Rent it, laugh at it, return it, and never think of it again.
Most of the details present in the Dreamcast version were sacrificed, replaced with only a few minor interface improvements.
For all Iggy's trademark puzzle-game annoyances, it also has the perks.
This game falls short of the mark and only makes you wonder how you could have liked fishing in the first place.

games.title: Indiana Jones and the Infernal Machine
games.index_title: Indiana Jones and the Infernal Mach

review.blurb: Indiana Jones and the Infernal Machine is a big game, to be sure, with enough levels to keep you busy over a three-day rental period.
If you can overlook the muddy graphics, Indy Racing 2000 is surprisingly fun.
Overall, International Superstar Soccer 64 offers just about everything FIFA 64 did. It just does it slightly better.
After you've beaten the game several times, it's easy to see why Konami wanted this title to go straight to rental.
"Was this really necessary?" is the question that Acclaim's Jeremy McGrath Supercross 2000 begs.
Nintendo 64 fans should pick up this title without delay and hope that Rareware's team of top-notch talent will continue to churn out even more games like this one for years to come.
Ken Griffey Jr.'s Slugfest, as the name implies, is a fast-paced arcade-style baseball game that will appeal to anyone who wants an intuitive, easy-to-play game.
KI Gold may lack that final finesse, but it more than makes up for it with fun.
While some might be initially put off by the youthful nature of Kirby 64, the depth of the power combo system really brings a lot to what would otherwise be an average platformer.
If you can remember Pyramid Patrol or Sewer Shark, you know exactly what kind of game it is: a 3D railed shooter.
Playing as the real guys is a kick, but so little technique is required of you that the game quickly falls flat.
Courtside easily adds up to the best basketball game on the N64, which, unfortunately, isn't much of a feat.
Majora's Mask is a great game, but it isn't for everybody.

games.title: The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time
games.index_title: Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time

review.blurb: Ocarina of Time definitely follows in the footsteps of the previous games, and the result is a game that can't be called anything other than flawless.
With bland gameplay, boring racing and a frustrating Lego construction system, Lego Racers just translates into an incomplete package.
Lode Runner 3-D is a major departure from the gameplay that has served the Lode Runner series so well for more than a decade.
Mace looks fantastic but still plays poorly.
Keeps the series' gameplay intact while drastically improving the graphics.
The game is by far the best version of Madden football on the N64 yet.
Madden NFL 2001 for the N64 is the most complete and refined version of the game thus far.
Fans will find more than enough reason to get the latest and best version of Madden football we've seen on the N64 yet.
The game gives you everything you could ever want on the features side, plus the ability to change them.
Magical Tetris Challenge does offer a degree of the familiar cataleptic state puzzle game fans are so fond of, but if you already own a Tetris game, you probably don't need to buy this one.

games.title: Major League Baseball Featuring Ken Griffey, Jr.

review.blurb: It's from Nintendo, and it has Ken Griffey Jr.'s endorsement.
Although Mario Golf is a little on the cutesy side, anyone who wants a great golf game need look no further.
Even with all its bells and whistles, Mario Kart 64 is very likely to disappoint you after about a week.
Mario Party is intended to be played with a group of friends, and for those people who can really take advantage of that, this game is a must-buy.

The greatest betterment is that since you won't get sick of the minigames as you did with the original, there's much more replay value in Mario Party 2, and replay value is the main point of the game.
Hudson's dedication to solid, addictive puzzle gaming that makes for a wholly enjoyable multiplayer experience is apparent.
Mario Tennis is a quality tennis presentation that strikes gold on the three points that matter most: value, gameplay, and fun.
Those who missed out on the PlayStation version could still find a good bit of entertainment value here, but this dated title simply can't compete on the same level as current software.

With 20 tracks, four battle arenas, and a plenitude of hidden characters and cheats to unlock, Mickey's Speedway USA has enough substance to keep even the most dedicated kart racing fanatics coming back for more.
Any of you seriously craving Micro Machines action on your Nintendo probably won't be too disappointed.
While the gameplay is on par with the original versions of the games, the original versions managed to look and sound a bit better than Midway's N64 renditions.
While Milo's Astro Lanes gets a resounding nod of approval for its daring immersion into subculture (albeit, three years too late), there's very little here to recommend a purchase.
Mischief Makers is a good game that will leave players wanting more.
Mission: Impossible's main problems stem from its inconsistency from level to level.
Why does the world need another F-1 simulation?
Gamers itchin' for a monster-truck fix should save their cash until the real thing rolls into town.
Fans of the arcade version won't be disappointed at all.
This one is only for the hard-core MK fans who don't already own PlayStations.
Mortal Kombat Trilogy is the last gasp of this 2D game.
It ultimately does what few updated classics do well, bringing new concepts to a well known game while keeping the fun and magic of the original game.
The bottom line is that MRC doesn't move fast enough to give the illusion of speed, making gameplay dull.
Mystical Ninja is a solid, quirky, and entirely rehashed title that holds its own well against the horde of painful Mario clones.
Nagano '98 isn't entirely bleak.
It's likely that you'll find at least one game among the six that will make the collection worth picking up for you, but it could certainly be all of them.

For the casual driving-game fan that almost likes NASCAR racing but never has bothered to watch an entire race, you may want to think about the 50 laps you have to race before taking that victory lap.
NASCAR 2000 is a better version of last year's title with more than enough new options and features to warrant a purchase by anyone who already owns NASCAR 99.
The arcade mode is a huge bonus that by itself is almost worth the price of the game.
Unless you've been shooting digital hoops in a cave for the past few years, you've seen it all before.
NBA In The Zone isn't much of a basketball game by any standards.
In the Zone '99 can deliver fun gameplay, especially with multiplayer games and three-point shooting contests, if gamers accept its defensive AI and speed flaws.
Those looking for a well-rounded hoops sim won't go wrong with the solidly built NBA Jam 99.
Dedicated games like NBA Showtime and NBA Live 2000 do a much better job of representing their respective schools of video-game basketball.
Fans of previous versions of Live N64 will have a good time.
The NBA season may be in jeopardy, but Live is open for business and always ready for a game.
Give it a three-day rental, and see if you're left hungry for more.
If you are an Evangelion fan and you already have the videos and an N64, then this game is probably for you. But be forewarned - it's for no one else.
The multiplayer mode is where this game pays off, specifically the three- and four-player games.
NFL Blitz is the best football game ever made.
People who haven't seen Blitz '99 in arcades yet are going to be blown away by the additions and tweaks that have been made since the original Blitz was released on the N64.

Once again, Midway delivers a rock-solid version of NFL Blitz for the Nintendo 64, equaling the PlayStation release in every respect while standing shoulder-to-shoulder with the Dreamcast in a number of key areas.
Playing NFL QB Club for the Nintendo 64 is much like watching a preseason football game; the choppy, sloppy gameplay soon grates on your patience.
Some will enjoy Quarterback Club despite its shortcomings
The story of Acclaim's NFL Quarterback Club 99 is a tale of two games.
Madden 2000 for the N64 is simply a better game.
NHL 99 retains the outstanding gameplay of the original 16-bit games.
Breakaway 98 can easily be called the best hockey game on the N64.
NHL 99 blows it out of the water.
It's really great to see a port of a game not make all the mistakes the original did, and it makes the title much easier to recommend.
Just start out playing it on the hard difficulty to ensure you get your money's worth.
Anyone who is interested in this game shouldn't be.
Ogre Battle 64 offers a detailed branching story and great depth of customization, both executed brilliantly within the limitations of a 35-meg cartridge.
The only possible way I could recommend this game is if you're an avid Olympic hockey fan.
With stunning graphics, excellent sound, and extremely solid gameplay, Paper Mario is a quality game on all fronts.
While a decent game on its own, Paper Boy 64 doesn't capture the magic of the original arcade game.
Mario Kart 64 or Diddy Kong Racing are still better bets, since the Choro-Q 64 engine is showing its age and showing it badly.
As a single-player or multiplayer FPS experience Perfect Dark is unparalleled on the console systems.
Dated graphics, gameplay flaws, and meager extras all conspire to bring down an otherwise average golf game.
The graphics, sound, control, and all around excitement add up to make Pilotwings 64 one hell of a great ride.
At first glance Pokémon Puzzle League appears to be the continued exploitation of the popular Pokémon property, there's actually a decent puzzle game to be found here as well.
Pokemon Snap is a must-buy for fans of the license and probably warrants interest from those who aren't.
If you're after a little more storyline and a little less generic RPG battling, Stadium comes off as a bit of a disappointment.
If you do have a Transfer Pak, want to get more out of Gold or Silver, and enjoy a lot of battling, then Stadium 2 is a complete package and is definitely worth picking up.
Unless you're desperate for anything snowmobile-ish, keep a safe distance away from this dud.
Powerful Pro Baseball 4 is a solid first attempt at an N64 baseball game.
Quake's masonry environments and gangly monsters seem right at home in N64 territory.
If you've never sat down with the PC version, you'll probably be able to easily overlook the game's problems.
Its primary flaw is oversimplicity on all fronts.
Everything has a striking tinge of realism.
Rally Challenge 2000 is, at best, a marginal attempt to bring the excitement of off-road rally racing to the Nintendo 64.
Rampage World Tour didn't exactly set the world on fire, and Rampage 2: Universal Tour doesn't bring along enough lighter fluid to warrant picking it up.
Unless you were a freak for the original game, you'll tire of RWT very quickly.
A truly excellent game on all fronts, Rayman 2 proves that sometimes you have to take a little time to do something right.
Acclaim has created a video-game version of R/C car racing that imitates reality all too well.
While the Nintendo 64 version of R2R puts on a decent show, the game is a bit on the sluggish side.
Ready 2 Rumble: Round 2 is a fun game that will undoubtedly impress those who've never played the original.
If you've already played the PlayStation or PC versions of RE2, there's really no reason to buy this game, but for N64 owners who haven't tried yet, it's an absolute must-have.
Ridge Racer 64 has to be one of the best racing games ever to hit Nintendo's system.
Yes, hitting people while driving a motorcycle is that much fun.
If you're itching to feel the breeze in your hair as you jam through a country track in your drop-top roadster, you may want to give this one a rental.
Maybe Midway knew what it was doing when it dropped this project.
Its gameplay is diverse yet solid, and it all combines to form a game that will keep you interested for quite some time.
Rush 2 attempts to embody the spirit of San Francisco Rush but fails to deliver as a sequel and a game.
There really isn't much reason to look this title up over the rapidly growing number of futuristic racers on the N64.
It's a decent port of an excellent arcade game.
Featuring a complete and functional version of the arcade game, plus a smattering of extra modes, Rush 2049's package is definitely the one to emulate.
Shadow Man embodies the spirit of solid-gold game design: a worthwhile, captivating storyline; gameplay that lets you choose your own path; and tons of hidden areas to discover.

games.title: Shadowgate 64: Trial of the Four Towers
games.index_title: Shadowgate 64: Trial of the Four To

review.blurb: The lack of any prolonged contact with other characters in the game and the repetitive musical score doesn't only make the game feel boring but lonely as well.

games.title: Sin and Punishment: Successor of the Earth
games.index_title: Sin and Punishment: Successor of th

review.blurb: IMPORT: Sin and Punishment is a lot of fun, but the game's complete lack of difficulty and fairly short length really hurt it.
SK2 might be the best party-racing alternative to Mario Kart 64.
The license goes a long way to keep the game and story interesting, but it's frustrating that the game itself wasn't better.
South Park Rally is a poor racing game that tries to capture the look and feel of the show with a few cute quips and phrases that become annoying after just a couple of races.
The repeating questions, repeating speech, and dull minigames keep it from being a game you'd want to pop in whenever you need a simple party-style game.
Space Invaders is a game different enough to cancel any feelings of nostalgia, yet it isn't good enough to stand on its own.
Ultimately, SSSV is a simple, moderately short game, with extremely engaging "personalities" that hit the game character target right on the mark.
Though the game isn't perfect, it is a wonderful accomplishment on the part of the developers, and it is simply very fun to play.
Star Fox 64 is the kind of game you will spend many sleepless nights with.

games.title: Star Wars: Episode I Battle for Naboo
games.index_title: Star Wars: Episode I Battle for Nab

review.blurb: Even with the graphical faults and the general lack of innovation over its predecessor, Battle for Naboo is still a fun mission-based shooter, as well as one of the best Episode I titles to hit the market yet.
It's even better than the scene that inspired it.
Any fan of the first level of Shadows of the Empire really should check out Rogue Squadron.

games.title: Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire
games.index_title: Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire

review.blurb: Problems with the control, camera angles, and frustrating save feature keep Shadows from reaching its full potential.
The huge number of levels available and the two-player split-screen mode - slowdown ridden as it is - make for an incredible amount of value.
For every thrill it offers in innovative gameplay, it stinks up the fun with graphics and control glitches.
Sumo Battle is an action/sports simulation that tries to depict every aspect of what a sumo wrestler must go through.
With realms so vast and detailed, and yet so graphically clean and simple, one instinctively wants to go exploring.
If you've got a crew of friends ready to pick a Nintendo character and throw down, then Super Smash Bros. is definitely worth a purchase.
Turning problems aside, the game features a pretty nice physics model, and the bikes buckle and bounce in a realistic fashion after big jumps.
It serves no purpose other than to firmly establish the bottom of the barrel.
Even if movie-license games turn you off, this game is an exception and worth taking a look at.
Tetrisphere is precisely the sort of game that the N64 really needs.
Tom & Jerry: Fists of Furry is a game that is accessible enough for the younger crowd, yet it still packs enough challenge and quality production that more mature gamers will appreciate it too.
If this game is remembered at all, it will be as the game that's not Rayman 2.

The Nintendo 64 port, handled by Edge of Reality, mimics the PlayStation version just about as well as it possibly can, and the resulting game is just as amazing as it was when it appeared on the PlayStation last September.
Tony Hawk's Pro Skater 2 represents nothing less than the best skateboarding game the system has to offer.
Stubborn N64 owners out there should take notice of Tony Hawk's Pro Skater 3, as it represents one of the best skateboarding games the platform has to offer.
The multiplayer aspect alone raises the score, but Top Gear Overdrive's rough edges really keep this game from maximizing on all Snowblind's hard work.
Top Gear Rally is a title of some inarguable merit.
Top Gear Rally 2 makes some definite improvements over the original, and while it doesn't cater to all the realism that hard-core rally fans expect, it certainly proves to be a good little racer.
Whether you've seen the movie or not, Toy Story 2, as a game, stands on its own because it offers gameplay.
Triple Play 2000 for the N64 is a watered-down version of last year's Triple Play for the PlayStation.
It's a landmark shooter and a must-buy.
Shadow of Oblivion is successful because it concentrates upon what made the Turok franchise a best-seller instead of attempting to one-up the competition, making it in many ways the best Turok yet.
Turok is more than just another Doom clone.
Even on the most difficult setting, the bot AI can't stand up to any steady-handed human player.
In the end, Twisted Edge turned out to be too little, too late.
Even the most hard-core rally fan should be hesitant around this title.
Vigilante 8 was one of the most celebrated car combat games on the PlayStation, and I'm pleased to say that with new multiplayer options and improved graphics the N64 version is even better.

manual_fix.note: Metadata refreshed from the 2004 index capture; component scores confirmed from the matching 2004 review capture.

review.blurb: The graphics in V8SO look much the same as in the last game, save for more impressive explosions, a few minor bits of eye candy, and a greater variety of environments in the levels.
The 3D view's lack of visual clarity makes for a frustrating game.
It's the video game equivalent of a college textbook: great to learn from but not really exciting to read.
Golf fans waiting to get a taste on the N64 can start with the clapping and cheering, because Nintendo has finally brought home a golf game to its flagship system.
War Gods is an interesting footnote to the MK legacy.
The graphics are better than what you'll find in 95 percent of games, and the analog stick makes play control a breeze.
Wayne Gretzky for the Nintendo 64 is one incredible title.
It's hard to recommend a wrestling game that doesn't contain any actual in-the-ring wrestling.
Despite its flaws, WCW Mayhem still manages to be a fun, original game, and it is definitely worth picking up.
WCW Nitro is just a rehash of the PlayStation version, only it's about a year too late.
You may want to rent WCW vs. NWO before purchasing it.
Remember WCW vs. NWO: World Tour, THQ's last Nintendo 64 wrestling game? Revenge builds on the previous game but makes most of the same mistakes.
Those who loved Tetrisphere will probably like Wetrix, and that those who hated Tetrisphere will probably like Wetrix as well.
The game's multiple flaws outweigh its good points and turns what could have been the next GoldenEye 007 into another boring shooter.
WipeOut 64 isn't horrible, it just feels like the developer's first effort for the system at times - which it is.
If nothing else, Wonder Project J2 will make you realize just how different the US and Japanese game markets truly are.
Die-hard soccer fanatics will find that the added tactical possibilities of World Cup 98 are worth the ticket price.
World Driver Championship is an average racing game with exceptional graphics.
The World Is Not Enough provides an engaging experience but fails to match the gameplay finesse of GoldenEye.
Worms Armageddon is not only the best Worms game, it's also an incredible multiplayer experience.
The most obvious improvement to the game is the roster, which has been updated with the times to reflect the ever-changing faces of the World Wrestling Federation.

If you're still drooling over Wrestlemania 2000, then the roster updates, graphical refinements, bonus secrets, and new modes found in WWF No Mercy should be more than enough to keep you going for another year.
If graphics and gameplay modes are all you care about, the N64 version is the way to go.
If you've played Revenge, you already know exactly what to expect from Wrestlemania 2000.

games.title: Xena: Warrior Princess - The Talisman of Fate

review.blurb: This would probably make a decent party game for players who don't know how to do anything more than mash every button available, but fans of fighting games should stay far, far away.
XG2 is not even close to being as impressive a game as the first one was when it came out back in '97.
Yoshi's Story is good for a rental at best.
